p: Body in the Bunker, The (19xx)(B. Trevelyan)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Body in the Bunker, The (19xx)(B. Trevelyan)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Body in the Bunker, The (19xx)(B. Trevelyan)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: The Body In The Bunker
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Body in the Bunker, The (19xx)(B. Trevelyan)[h TSTH][bootfile]-marquee.png

meta.name: Boffin
meta.desc: The player must guide the Boffin through a series of caverns by moving him around the platforms and obstacles on the levels. The Boffin can only jump short distances, but he can fall from any height by opening his umbrella to slow his descent to a safe speed. Each level ends when the player touches an owl which is usually located at the bottom of the screen. The 'lucky' owl can only be touched without resulting in death if all of the 'unlucky' upside-down horseshoes have been destroyed.

meta.name: Boffin V.2
meta.desc: Boffin is a platform game developed by Paul Julian O'Malley and published by Addictive Games. The aim of Professor Boffin is to pass through a series of caves as quickly as possible by destroying all of the unlucky horseshoes in each cavern and then touching the resident owl, guardian of each cave. There are two different versions of Boffin on the tape. Boffin v.2 is recorded first, followed by v.1. Boffin v.2 runs on the Acorn Electron and also the BBC Micro. It has 25 different caverns. The first version of Boffin runs on the BBC Micro and features high resolution graphics and 20 caverns - all completely different from those in Boffin v.2.

meta.name: 3D Bomb Alley
meta.desc: A realistic 3D simulation of an explosive Air-Sea Battle in which you must defend a fleet of ships from attacking enemy aircraft.

meta.name: Bomber Baron
meta.desc: As the renowned pilot "Baron Von Rippoffen", it is your duty to destroy the enemy landscape. Loaded with bombs, your plane will fly over the cities. It's up to you to destroy them completely and land, thus paving the way for reinforcements. Sounds simple, eh? Just you try it.

meta.name: Bone Cruncher
meta.desc: Bonecruncher is a computer game for the Acorn Electron, BBC Micro, Commodore 64 and Amiga first published by Superior Software in 1987. It is a puzzle game which uses the 'rocks and diamonds' mechanics of Boulder Dash.

meta.name: Boot Hill
meta.desc: Take the role of Buffalo Bill or Big Bad Tom in this Western shootout for two players. Shoot your opponent five times to win the game and be declared the best gunslinger in the West.

meta.name: Bored Of The Rings
meta.desc: The Great Ring needs to be returned to Mount Doom, a treacherous task to be undertaken by Fordo the Boggit. Spam, Pimply and Murky are his country-bumpkin friends, and he will later meet with Bumbadil and Hashberry, two old hippies. The main quest involves meeting up with Legoland the Elf, Giblet the Dwarf and Aragont. In keeping with the humour, familiar locations become "the featureless plain below the pink mountain", "Lake Anadin" and the "Land of the Personal-Stereo-Wearing Goblins". Bored of the Rings is an interactive fiction game with graphics made using Quill. It was written by Fergus McNeill and Ian Willis. The interface allows the player to take and use items using VERB NOUN commands, and talk to characters. The game is split into three parts, accessed via passcodes. The tape also includes issue 1 of Sceptical, Delta 4's short-lived magazine on tape.

meta.name: Boris In The Underworld
meta.desc: Boris has had his home raided by the underworld creatures and they have stolen most of his possessions. As you can imagine, Boris is quite upset about this and so he has decided to venture into the underworld and retrieve all his valuables. Your task is to guide Boris through the underworld and fight off the underworld creatures.

meta.name: Boulder Dash
meta.desc: Eres Rockford, y tienes que cavar a través de cuevas infestadas de monstruos en busca de diamantes. En cada nivel debes reunir un número concreto de diamantes para abrir un portal al siguiente nivel. Los enemigos pueden ser aplastados por la caída de rocas, que quedan sueltas cuando se quita el suelo debajo de ellas o se empujan sobre un espacio vacío, pero ten cuidado porque tambien pueden aplastarte a ti. En los niveles más avanzados, la dificultad aumenta añadiendo más rompecabezas y límites de tiempo más cortos.

meta.name: Bouncer
meta.desc: Bouncer is a game in the style of Atari's Missile Command, which requires you to catch Space Bouncers in the centre of a circle of anti-matter that takes the place of the original's cross hairs. If you take too long, the anti-matter circle hits the ground or a Bouncer hits the side of the circle then a life is lost.

meta.name: Bouncer
meta.desc: Bounce Arthur around the pyramid of cubes, changing the colour of the top surface of each cube as he lands on it. When all the curbes have changed colour you will receive a bonus and be given a new pyramid to change. The game starts on level one, and each time you successfully complete four pyramids you will move up to the next level. At level one, landing on a cube once will change it to the target colour, but at level two you will have to land on each cube twice. At level three you should try to bounce once only on each cube; a second bounce will restore it to its original colour, and leave you where you started.

meta.name: Bouncing Bill
meta.desc: Rescue The Damsons ! For all you chivalrous knights of the 21st century a new challenge has been launched. This time it is not some fair maiden that has to be rescued from a high tower but a basket of damsons (in distress!). This is the plot behind Bouncing Bill from Oak Software. You are given three lives to negotiate a conveyer belt system of moving walls by jumping through holes in them. Your task is to reach the top of the screen and seize the basket of damsons suspended above, with points awarded for each wall passed. Double points are lost for a retreat, accidental or otherwise. Simple? It is at first, but the added bonus you earn begins to be paid for as ghosts haunt their way about the screen.

p: Bounty Bob Strikes Back (1986)(Micro Power)[h TSTH][t +2][bootfile].zip
n: Bounty Bob Strikes Back (1986)(Micro Power)[h TSTH][t +2][bootfile]

meta.name: Bounty Bob Strikes Back!
meta.desc: Bounty Bob returns in this new and exciting follow-up adventure to Miner 2049er. This time around it's even tougher than before and Bob needs your help more than ever to guide him through the mine. The mutant organisms have multiplied and over-run the mine entirely, making it extremely difficult to survive the hazards of the underground passageways. Using the high powered special equipment in the twenty caverns is Bob's only hope of achieving his objective of securing the mine and defeating the plans of the evil Yukon Yohan.

meta.name: The Box of Treasures
meta.desc:
Box Of Treasures contains five programs related to the theme of boxes, though each can be used in a variety of situations.
- You explore to discover your task, but the idea is to rescue Henry, find the magician and choose from three possible endings. There are no red herrings, blind alleys or deaths. Children thought it superb, apart from the cursor controls being the wrong way round, as in pressing the up key to move down.
- Box Draw produces complex eight colour designs by drawing up to 100 rectangles. You can fill in, half fill, outline or delete, the scale can be altered and picture position changed.
- Blank is a most useful utility with endless applications - you can design forms containing up to 40 vertical lines, 40 horizontal lines and 30 text strings in a choice of three line thicknesses and two text heights.
- Make-a-Box can be used as an introduction to a boxes project, or simply to stimulate the imagination. Choose the characteristics and details of a box, and the design is drawn on screen.
The two former programs have editing facilities and the results of all three can be saved, loaded or dumped to a printer. Create is the weakest part of an excellent package. Stories can be created with static or animated Mode 7 illustrations, but producing anything worth while is time consuming.

meta.name: Boxer
meta.desc:
You and your rival are competing for the attentions of a young lady. At the botton of the screen, she is releasing ballons which you must try to catch when they get stuck in the rafters of the Gym. Any ballons which escape to the top of the screen will be awarded to your oponent.
If you collect five balloons before he does, your score will be increased by the bonus in the top right-hand corner of the screen, and you will move to a more challenge Gym.
If your opponent is the first one to collect five ballons, you will lose a life and stay in the same Gym.

meta.name: Brain Aerobics
meta.desc: BRAIN AEROBICS was the first computer "BRAIN TRAINING" program for home computers. developed 1985/1986. It set 4 basic logic based puzzles all based on playing cards with many variations to test the brain. The ulitmate test CULBERTON'S REVENGE (with 13 cards) is legendary and well worth a try.

meta.name: Breakthrough
meta.desc: Trapped in a world of fire and energy, you must battle for the most valuable prize of all - your life. You can escape only by using your magical powers to destroy the rocks that block your path or to create new blocks that act as stepping stones on the road to freedom. Don't forget to pick up the key to the exit door - otherwise your efforts will be in vain. "There are 100 screens just waiting to trap, tease and test your powers of perception before you finally break through to the outside world.

meta.name: Breakout
meta.desc: Keep the ball in play by hitting it with the bat, scoring points for each bricks in the different layers are worth different amounts. You can control the angle at which the ball will bounce by hitting it with different sections of the bat.

meta.name: Breakout (IJK)
meta.desc: Breakout for 1 or 2 players with 6 difficulty levels. Knock the bricks out of the wall.

meta.name: Brian Clough's Football Fortunes
meta.desc:
Brian Clough's Football Fortunes is a football management computer board game suitable for two to five players. 
Each player takes on the role of a club manager. The object of the game is for each manager to successfully steer his team through the season using his skill to make his team stronger as the game proceeds. Each manager competes with human and computer controlled teams in a ten strong league and the F.A. Cup. From the second season onwards, each team will also enter a European competition (League Champions to the European Cup, F.A. Cup Winners to the Cup-Winners' Cup, and the remaining clubs will go to the UEFA Cup). 
The winner of Brian Clough's Football Fortunes is the manager who survives to the end of the game and has the highest number of game points. Game points are points awarded by the computer program, based on each managers performance in League and Cup competitions and on the amount of cash the player has left at the end of the game.

p: Brian Jack's Superstar Challenge (1985)(Martech)[h TSTH][bootfile-copy protected].zip
n: Brian Jack's Superstar Challenge (1985)(Martech)[h TSTH][bootfile-copy protected]

meta.name: Brian Jacks Superstar Challenge
meta.desc:
Brian Jack's Superstar Challenge es un juego deportivo multievento con licencia del medallista de bronce olímpico británico de 1972 Brian Jacks. En este juego, compites contra Brian en ocho pruebas diferentes y obtienes puntos en ellas en función de tu rendimiento. Las pruebas son carrera a pie, tiro con arco, ciclismo, fútbol, natación, sentadilla, piragüismo y pulseada.
No hay que batir ningún tiempo ni puntuación de clasificación y compites en todas las pruebas independientemente de tu rendimiento. El juego se desarrolla en varios niveles y si consigues más puntos que Brian, ganas el nivel y pasas al siguiente, donde se repite el proceso. A continuación, las puntuaciones de cada nivel se combinan para obtener una puntuación total. Si Brian consigue más puntos que tú al final de un nivel, pierdes el desafío.

meta.name: Brick 'em In
meta.desc: Catch the people eaters ba walling them in so they can't move. You may push any number of bricks or just move. All you have to fo is top the monsters chasing you around the screen by placing obstacles in their way. Sounds simple doesn't it ? But brick them in ... or else.

p: Bridge Mentor II v1.1 (1986)(Minic Business Services)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Bridge Mentor II v1.1 (1986)(Minic Business Services)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Bridge Mentor II v1.1 (1986)(Minic Business Services)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: Bridge Mentor II
meta.desc: Bridge Mentor II is the third reincarnation of a program first released in 1984. You can play out the existing archive of deals inthegamelibrarywhich comes with the program. Sixty deals are contained in the library and range from simple house rules conventions to the more complex conventions used in the Bridge-playing world.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bridge Mentor II v1.1 (1986)(Minic Business Services)[h TSTH][bootfile]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bridge Mentor II v1.1 (1986)(Minic Business Services)[h TSTH][bootfile]-marquee.png

p: Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: Play Your Cards Right
meta.desc: A higher-or-lower card game combined with general knowledge and survey questions. Based on the UK highly successful TV game of the same name. The TV game is a "London Weekend Television Production", made in assocation with Mark Goodson and Talbot Television, hosted by Bruce Forsyth and adapted for your home computer. It is a game for two players, or a single player playing against the computer, attempting to achieve maximum points by doubling your points on the turn of a card, just like the TV game.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile]-image.jpg
meta.video: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/videos/Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile]-video.mp4
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right (1986)(Britannia)[h TSTH][bootfile]-marquee.png

meta.name: Bruce Lee
meta.desc:
En Bruce Lee, el objetivo del personaje es llegar hasta un hechicero en una guarida subterránea, cuya derrota puede ofrecer riquezas incalculables.
En cada zona, debes recoger los numerosos faroles estratégicamente colocados antes de salir por el pasadizo recién revelado. Algunas zonas también cuentan con escaleras mecánicas y las clásicas escaleras de mano. Hay 20 zonas que completar.
Por el camino, te enfrentarás a dos enemigos: un ninja y un guerrero de sumo verde llamado Yamo. Puedes derrotarlos dándoles puñetazos, patadas, dejándoles caer sobre sus cabezas, atrayéndoles hacia terrenos peligrosos, ... Sin embargo, incluso después de matarlos, vuelven de nuevo.

meta.name: Bubble Bobble
meta.desc:
Bubble Bobble es la historia de dos humanos, Bub y Bob, que deambulan por la misteriosa cueva de los monstruos y mágicamente se convierten en dinosaurios. La única forma de transformarse es llegar al final de la cueva ...
Bubble Bobble es un juego de plataformas, con cada nivel en una sola pantalla. Los enemigos deben ser eliminados de un nivel para pasar al siguiente. Con un jugador controlando a Bub y el otro controlando a Bob, el jugador puede saltar y recoger elementos para obtener puntos. Sin embargo, el verdadero poder que tienen Bub y Bob es la capacidad de hacer burbujas. Estas burbujas pueden ser plataformas para saltar o atrapar enemigos. Los enemigos atrapados en una burbuja deben explotar saltando sobre ellos, en donde se convertirán en fruta. Además, los power-ups a veces flotan en burbujas. Incluyen relámpagos, que vuelan horizontalmente hacia los enemigos, y agua, que arrastra al jugador y a los enemigos hacia abajo, fluyendo sobre las plataformas.

p: Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show (1989)(Tynesoft)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show (1989)(Tynesoft)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.name: Buffalo Bill's Rodeo Games
meta.desc: Buffalo Bill's Wild West Show (known as Buffalo Bill's Rodeo Games in Europe) is a collection of six Wild West themed mini-games. You can play as many or as few of these as you like, and can always have another go at them if you fail. Games include Knife Throwing, Trick Shooting, Calf Roping and Bronco Riding. The games were programmed by Kevin Blake and Jason Sobell. The original 1989 release was by Tynesoft with a re-release in 1992 by Microvalue

meta.name: Bugblaster
meta.desc:
Inspired by the arcade game Centipede, Bug Blaster is a single screen shooter where you must destroy a Centipede that moves from the top of the screen. The Centipede moves across the screen and when it reaches the other side it moves down one place and moves back across the screen. You control a spacecraft at the bottom of the screen shooting the Centipede whilst moving left or right, as well being able to move forwards and backwards a short distance. Shooting the Centipede a number of times splits it into two and the other parts split into two when shot a number of times. 
Scattered around the screen are mushrooms and when the Centipede hits them then it changes directions but the mushrooms can be shot to make them disappear and receive points. Other creatures make an appearance and need shooting or avoiding and they are a insect that places more mushrooms, spiders, scorpions and snails and if you are touched by any creatures then you lose one of three lives. Destroying all parts of the Centipede moves you to the next level.

meta.name: Bugblaster
meta.desc:
Inspired by the arcade game Centipede, Bug Blaster is a single screen shooter where you must destroy a Centipede that moves from the top of the screen. The Centipede moves across the screen and when it reaches the other side it moves down one place and moves back across the screen. You control a spacecraft at the bottom of the screen shooting the Centipede whilst moving left or right, as well being able to move forwards and backwards a short distance. Shooting the Centipede a number of times splits it into two and the other parts split into two when shot a number of times. 
Scattered around the screen are mushrooms and when the Centipede hits them then it changes directions but the mushrooms can be shot to make them disappear and receive points. Other creatures make an appearance and need shooting or avoiding and they are a insect that places more mushrooms, spiders, scorpions and snails and if you are touched by any creatures then you lose one of three lives. Destroying all parts of the Centipede moves you to the next level.

meta.name: Bug Bomb
meta.desc: Bug Bomb is a tingling new arcade game by Simon Birrell. In the year 2184, men were sent down into the Earth to wander the electronic grids that provided the power supplies for the city. The task of these men was simple but difficult - to rid the grids of bugs that lived there, feeding on the currents! Henry was one such man - help him defeat the bugs!

meta.name: Bug Eyes (AKA Zelda Meets The Bug Eyes)
meta.desc: In this platformer the player character Space Agent Zelda must save the earth from the Xxabaneans (nicknamed the Bug Eyes).

p: Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: Bug Eyes II
meta.desc: Light years from Earth lies the rusting hulk of the Xxabanean flagship, disabled when it's power generator was destroyed by Space Agent Zelda. But Zelda was captured and imprisoned in the depths of a distant and desolate asteroid. Somewhere hidden on the rusting ship are the 25 keys that are needed to free Zelda from her intergalactic prison. Your mission, as Space Agent Starman, is to penetrate the defences of the ship and find the 25 keys in order to rescue Zelda. Some of the old Xxanabean booby traps are still in operation, and strange alien creatures have moved into the empty hulk. You have only five refills of oxygen. Your limited supply is depleted more quickly by collisions with objects and creatures. You must find the key before it runs out.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile]-image.jpg
meta.video: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/videos/Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile]-video.mp4

meta.thumbnail: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile]-thumb.jpg
meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Bug Eyes 2 - Starman to the Rescue (19xx)(Audiogenic)[h TSTH][bootfile]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Bullseye
meta.desc: Bullseye is a version of the successful TV quiz programme, adapted for popular home computers. It is a game for two players, played over four rounds. Skill at answering questions from any of nine different categories is combined with simulated dart throwing to gain high scores. A slightly tedious re-imagining of the TV show of the same name. Throw darts and answer general knowledge questions.

meta.name: Bumble Bee
meta.desc: You must guide Bumble around the maze, swinging the gates and eating the pollen grains as you go. But do not be lured by the tasty Toadstools or the bowls of gorgeous food for they contain poison! You may gain extra points by eating the mouth-wateringly large bundles of pollen and the fruit that appears periodically in the centre of the screen - but be careful that a voracious spider is not about to arrive on the scene! These spiders are your most hated enemy and pursue you relentlessly. They will catch you and kill you! They are immune to the poison in the Toadstools but not that in the bowls of food. Luckily, they are not very clever so you can trick them into eating this food. They die swiftly! You may pass from one side of the maze to the other by going through the tunnel. When you have eaten all the pollen, the gates leading to the matter transmitter (in the bottom left hand side of the maze) open so allowing you to progress to the next level. In the higher levels, spiders appear quicker and move faster so "Bee-ware"!

meta.name: Bun Fun
meta.desc: Operate the nozzles of a bun-finishing production line, creating as many good buns as possible.

meta.name: Business Games
meta.desc: Business Games contains two educational games. Although intended for use in business and economic studies, teaching the games are entertaining and thought-provoking in their own right. This guide describes the two programs that make up Business Games. STOKMARK, which simulates the conditions of the stock market, with realistic behaviour of share prices based on an analysis of real companies. In TELEMARK, four teams or players each control the finances of a firm that makes and sells televisions. In both these games each player in turn will be prompted either to select an option (for example, Yes or No) or to enter numbers or letters: the RETURN key should be pressed after each selection or input.

p: By Fair Means or Foul (1986)(Superior)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: By Fair Means or Foul (1986)(Superior)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.name: By Fair Means or Foul
meta.desc: On ST and Amiga features a full range of jabs, uppercuts and weaves. All fights take place to realistic WBA rules, as you aim to rise the rankings and fight for the title. The crowd frequently shout encouragement and insults, which are displayed using speech bubbles.

meta.name: Caesar The Cat
meta.desc: The screen displays a kitchen shelf laden with food and crockery. However, the little vermins nibble away at the food and under your guidance, Caesar must leap from shelf to shelf to catch the naughty rodents. Scoring system is unusual. You start with 10,000 points and it automatically goes down as time passes. However, score is gained by catching a mouse and dumping it in a larder for later consumption. A huge chunk of your score disappears if Caesar knocks down any crockery or bangs his head against the wall. The game ends if he knocks the red crockery off the shelf or if the score goes down to zero. When Caesar catches nine mice, a new set of greedier, nippier mice come on the scene but they're worth more points!

meta.name: Calligraphy
meta.desc: Font design and printing software for BBC

meta.name: Camelot
meta.desc: In Camelot, the player assumes the role of a deposed king, trying to prove his worthiness for the throne. The main goal of the game is to collect 3000 points by recovering bags of gold scattered around the flip-screen castle the game is set in. Killing enemies also increases the score, though in less amounts.

meta.name: Canyon
meta.desc: You are the pilot of a small biplane, and the sole remaining active defender of the little mountain kingdom of Asdel against the invading robot tyranny of the Empire of Xar... This colourful mind- and eye-riveting game puts you at the controls of an agile little biplane. You sweep down over river canyons thick with the powerfully armed fighters of is Electronic Highness, the Robot Emperor of Xar. You manoeuvre between the twisting walls of the canyons, trying to avoid crashing into them or the islands in your path and also avoid the Beamers, Bombers, Missile Boats and Proximity Mines. Just then you think it is safe to relax... you have to pass through the Gates of Fire; and then, battle-scarred and thinking yourself victorious, you encounter the Imperial Flagship.

meta.name: ZZZ(notgame):#NONGAME
meta.desc: Music Synthesiser turns the BBC Micro into a four channel sequencer, with three instrument channels and one effects channel. Facilities include: Entry and modification of musical pieces, Defining up to 16 instructions (only four available on OS 0.1), Saving and loading entire pieces on tape or disc, Playing pieces either straight through or using a verse structure. You are well advised to read the BBC manual's section on ENVELOPE before using Music Synthesiser.

meta.name: Carnival
meta.desc: Eliminate all targets moving in three rows, plus the eight rotating pipes at the top of the screen. The targets are rabbits, owls, ducks, numbered squares (which give extra bullets) and the letters BONUS which, if shot in order, give a bonus. You control a gun at the bottom of the screen. It's game over if you run out of bullets. Beware: There is an inexhaustible supply of ducks and if they get to the bottom row, they may fly down and eat some of your bullets.

meta.name: Carousel (AKA Carnival)
meta.desc:
Ducks, rabbits and owls are circulating above you. You have a gun and a limite supply of bullets which you must use to shoot them down.
Your supply of bullets is displayed along the bottom of the screen. Watch out for the ducks which swoop down - if they manage to land, they take away ten of your bullets.
Mixed in amongst the animals are the letters of the word "BONUS" - you gain a bonus score for each one you shoot down in the correct order.

meta.name: Roulette
meta.desc: Second game in IJK's CASINO program pack.

meta.name: Castle Assault
meta.desc: Castle Assault is an action platformer originally published by MRM Software in 1984 and re-released on the Blue Ribbon label in 1985. A glittering reward awaits the intrepid atop the heavily defended towering turrets of this forbidding fortress. On your quest for gold you will be assailed by a menacing menaderie of meanies, namely crabs, snakes, lobsters, spiders, bugs and beetles. Watch out for those malicious malades (the deadly Flying Ducks) as you dodge dalling rocks, scale ladders and negotiate moving platforms. Collect various different fruits for bonus points on your way. Bonus man for every two bags of gold collected.

meta.name: Castle Assault
meta.desc: Castle Assault is an action platformer originally published by MRM Software in 1984 and re-released on the Blue Ribbon label in 1985. A glittering reward awaits the intrepid atop the heavily defended towering turrets of this forbidding fortress. On your quest for gold you will be assailed by a menacing menaderie of meanies, namely crabs, snakes, lobsters, spiders, bugs and beetles. Watch out for those malicious malades (the deadly Flying Ducks) as you dodge dalling rocks, scale ladders and negotiate moving platforms. Collect various different fruits for bonus points on your way. Bonus man for every two bags of gold collected.

meta.name: Castle Blackstar
meta.desc:
Al despertar de un sueño atormentado por extraños sueños y visiones, te encuentras en una lujosa habitación amueblada con plata y cristal. Lo más llamativo de la habitación es la mujer que le habla. Es alta, pechugona e inspira autoridad y sabiduría. Vuelve a hablar: "... por último, cuando hayas localizado el orbe, debes devolvérmelo. Puedes quedarte con cualquier tesoro mortal que encuentres después de que lo haya limpiado de su maldad". Ella hace una pausa, y luego dice: "Adelante, cumple mis órdenes". Te inclinas mientras dices: "Lady Artemis..."
La oscuridad te envuelve, hasta que de repente despiertas a la luz del sol del Castillo Blackstar Valley. Tu búsqueda ha comenzado...

p: Castle Frankenstein - Epic Adventure 1 v2 (1984)(Epic)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Castle Frankenstein - Epic Adventure 1 v2 (1984)(Epic)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.name: Castle Frankenstein
meta.desc: Castle Frankenstein is a full-scale adventure in which you have to explore the ruins and grounds of Castle Frankenstein in order to find and destroy the Monster which he has created. It is nearly twenty years since the Frankenstein Monster was last seen or heard of. He is thought to have been destroyed in a fire at the Castle, but recently a series of unsolved murders has taken place in the nearby village, and the people fear that the Monster is on the loose again. They have chosen you as the one to put an end to the curse that has plagued them ever since Dr Frankenstein brought his hideous creation to life. Are you brave enough to explore the Castle, with its many dark tunnels and secret passages, and search for the Monster?

meta.name: Castle Of Gems
meta.desc: The idea of the game is for Bertie Bear to collect as many gems as possible that are scattered around the castle. When all the gems have been collected, Bertie moves on to the next of nine different screens. However, Bertie has got company in the shape of Gem Gobblers, Trees, Witches, Ghosts and Gremlins - all of whom are fatal if Bertie collides with any one of them. The Gem Gobblers, Trees and Gremlins are also after the gems so there's a bonus score if Bertie can collect the last gem on a screen. Now and again there's a Magic Hat to make Bertie invincible for a while and a Honey Pot for gaining bonus points. Most of the castles have moving lifts upon which only Bertie may ascend/descend to different levels. If Bertie takes too long on any screen the "baddies" he has killed previously will start to come to life again!

p: Castle of Riddles v1 (1982)(Acornsoft)[h TSTH][m savegame AJH][bootfile].zip
n: Castle of Riddles v1 (1982)(Acornsoft)[h TSTH][m savegame AJH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Castle of Riddles v1 (1982)(Acornsoft)[h TSTH][m savegame AJH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: Castle Of Riddles
meta.desc: The player takes the role of a 'professional adventurer' who is 'down to his last silver piece'. His services are hired by a wizard whose castle has been taken over by a warlock. The warlock has also found the wizard's Magic Ring of Power which must be returned. The player must navigate the castle, solve problems and avoid booby traps set by the warlock in order to recover the magic ring for the wizard. The adventurer must also search for treasure and deposit it in a safe which he can then keep as payment.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Castle of Riddles v1 (1982)(Acornsoft)[h TSTH][m savegame AJH][bootfile]-image.jpg

meta.name: Castle Quest
meta.desc: Castle Quest is a 1985 video game for the BBC Micro, of significant importance in the gaming history of the platform. It was noted at the time of release as being the best game on the platform in its genre for its problem solving, colourful graphics and smooth hardware scrolling. It has also been compared to Metroid.

meta.name: Caterpillar
meta.desc: "Arnold" is a hungry caterpillar who has lost his way. Your job is to eat all the lettuces in the garden and avoid the poisoned mushrooms. Bonuses are given if the allowed time for eating the lettuces is beaten and as the game progresses, more mushrooms and lettuces are added and the speed increases. As you become more skilful at the game, a second caterpillar called "Charlie" makes the game even more difficult since he must be avoided as well as the poisonous mushrooms if you are to avoid losing a life!

meta.name: Caterpillar
meta.desc: One of the earliest versions of the Centipede game, unusual for its green screen background. A reasonable blaster.

meta.name: Caveman Capers (AKA Caveman!)
meta.desc:
Caveman Capers is somewhat similar to BC's Quest for Tires.
In this side-scrolling platform game you control Ogg who is riding his turtle Kickstart. Jump over gaps, avoid dinosaurs, pterodactyls and other hazards.
Contains 60 levels.
meta.genre: Plataformas / Corre y Salta con desplazamiento

meta.name: Cells And Serpents
meta.desc: Cells And Serpents - the fantasy you won't survive! Go adventuring in the meanest, darkest, most definitely fatal set of dungeons you ever waved a sword around! There are more monsters in here than you ever dared to have nightmares about! Fancy meeting a Mind Flayer for instance? How about being sized up by an Intellect Devourer? If you live long enough (doubtful) you might just get to shake hands with Asmodeus! Only once, mind... There is great gold and glory to be had here, but be very, very careful where you tread! You can save your game onto tape at any point if it all gets too much and carry on once you've recovered your nerve... Find out how good you really are at adventure with this frantic fantasy. Not for the faint of heart or slow of sword! Cells And Serpents is an adventure game set in an underground labyrinth of rooms. Each of these might contain one of an enormous variety of monsters (which could kill you), treasure or artifacts (which will increase your wealth or magical and physical capabilities). Your aim is to collect as much treasure as possible by delving deeper and deeper into the maze... and staying alive!

meta.name: Centipede
meta.desc:
In Centipede, the player is trapped in the Enchanted Forest. Armed with only a magic wand to ward off the forest's insect denizens, all of which apparently are attacking in the player in continuous waves. 
The player must use the magic wand to shoot sparks at approaching insects to score points by pressing the controller button. Holding down the controller button will set the wand to rapid fire shots. If the player is bitten by an insect, the player will be temporarily paralyzed and lose one of the three starting magic wands.

meta.name: Centipede
meta.desc: You control Fred, from the local post control, and are faced with a terrible task. Armed only with your trusty spray gun, you must face a garden full of mutated mushrooms and bugs. Keep Fred alive as long as possible by spraying all the giant pests that occupy the garden. Defend yourself against the marauding centipede covered in poisonous hairs by shooting all the segments before you are touched.

meta.name: Centibug
meta.desc: The centibug descends from the top of the screen weaving intimidatingly between the mushrooms. Your objective is to shoot all the segments of the centibug before it reaches the bottom of the screen. Features include: spiders, snails, flies, six skill levels, hi-score, rankings, and increasing difficulty.

meta.name: Challenger
meta.desc: As controller of Challenger, your task is to destroy all aliens and quads. When you succeed in clearing a ground, you proceed to the next ground. There are four different grounds making up one level. Your fuel is continuously depleted, but can be restored by destroying fuel dumps. you lose a ship if you run out of fuel. Rockets, quads, pips and mutants are all out to get you though you have an unlimited supply of missiles and bombs and a limited supply of INVISO (shields) in your defence. Quads appear on levels 2-20; on levels 3-20 quads throw out pips. There is also WARP, which instantly moves you up three levels, but you'll have to find out how! There are over 200 levels with every 4th level being a dogfight between you and the quads and mutants.

meta.name: Champions!
meta.desc:
Take control of a football team in a division below the top one, and try to lead them to glory in both league and European competition. You start with a squad of 12 players, whose names can be changed, but have room for 15 players in total, with chances to buy and sell players. You can take out a bank loan, the level of which depends on your division.
The team's formation can be customised, and as outfield players are not limited to a single position, you can customise the team's strengths and weaknesses in quite a precise way. Matches are played out by listing scorers as they happen, and are followed by a full list of the division's other results. Finances change after each match to to wages, gate receipts, and possible incidents such as riots and illegal player approaches.

meta.name: The Land Of Chark
meta.desc: Welcome to "Chark", a traditional land of monsters & magic. You must find the lost treasure of the King and return it to him in the castle. You score can be increased by ridding the land of evil creatures.

meta.name: Chart Challenge '88 pop Quiz
meta.desc: Your musical knowledge is pushed to the limit in seven rounds. Your score is kept as a running total and is stored on disc when you complete the final section. "Options" is a multiple choice, question and answer game. A question - relating to your chosen decade - is displayed on the screen with two answers below it. Select correctly and you move on to the next question. This continues, with an increasing number of possible answers, until you answer incorrectly or the time limit expires. A much improved Hangman program constitutes round two, and here you have to guess both the single and the artist before the time limit expires and you get another go. "Link Lines" begins with a 3 x 3 grid of coloured squares, with each colour representing a different era. Your task is to answer three questions in a line, either vertically, horizontally or diagonally. Success is rewarded with a new grid of increased dimensions. Matching song titles to their artists, years released or highest chart position is the objective of Connections. As with all of the other games, the round ends with the first incorrect answer. A ladder is used to chart your progress in round five - True or False. By giving the correct response to a displayed statement, you are moved one rung up the ladder while the first wrong answer moves you down by one rung, a second failure by two rungs, and so on. As its name suggests, "Gold Run" bears more than a passing resemblance to a popular TV quiz game. The screen is filled with 20 hexagonal tiles each containing the initial letters of a song title. When you select a hexagon you are given the title, but with one word omitted - enter this to win the tile. To complete the round you must cross the screen from left to right before the time limit expires. "Options" returns in round seven, but this time you can specify the number of answers that you want displayed.

meta.name: Chauffeur
meta.desc: A mouse driver utility: Adapts almost all software to mouse control

meta.name: Checkout
meta.desc: You are in a square room which is divided into much smaller squares. Your sole aim is to change the colours of the smaller squares not once but twice. If you cross them a third time, they will go back to the original colour. It sounds easy - but it isn't. Not only is it difficult to run over all the tiles just twice but there is a remote-controlled robot also patrolling the room and, as the sands of time run out, he will fire a four-way death-dealing laser. See if you can chage the floor colour by running Checkout Charlie around the room without him being zapped.

meta.name: Chess
meta.desc: This chess-playing program helps the novice chess-player by its use of friendly interfaces, such as joysticks and cursor-keys, to get to grips with chess, while at the same time providing a game of increasing complexity on the higher levels for all further advanced players.

meta.name: Chess
meta.desc: Chess allows you to play a game of chess against either the computer or another player. All the rules of chess are included and the winner is the player that makes the other players King unable to move without being taken. Against the computer you are able to play as white or black and the computer has 10 skill levels (0-9) with zero being the easiest. You are also able to watch the computer play itself. There is also an Editor mode that allows you to set the board up with the pieces in any position you chose and you can attempt to get a mate in two or five moves as well.

meta.name: Chess
meta.desc: Chess allows you to play a game of chess against either the computer or another player. All the rules of chess are included and the winner is the player that makes the other players King unable to move without being taken. Against the computer you are able to play as white or black and the computer has 10 skill levels (0-9) with zero being the easiest. You are also able to watch the computer play itself. There is also an Editor mode that allows you to set the board up with the pieces in any position you chose and you can attempt to get a mate in two or five moves as well.

meta.name: Chess
meta.desc: This chess-playing program helps the novice chess-player by its use of friendly interfaces, such as joysticks and cursor-keys, to get to grips with chess, while at the same time providing a game of increasing complexity on the higher levels for all further advanced players.

meta.name: Chess
meta.desc: Evolution of the Superior chess game.

meta.name: Chess (Superior)
meta.desc: Es un juego de ajedrez básico con selección de nivel y velocidad. Los movimientos se introducen mediante texto, pero el resultado se muestra gráficamente en pantalla.

meta.name: Chicane
meta.desc: Chicane is a race simulation written with the experience of the drivers in the Kempston Micro Racing Team. The game is based on Formula Ford 2000, single seaters competing in the British Championship. Six famous Race tracks are featured, where the driver uses his skill to qualify in practice and then race with intelligent cars as opposition (Brands Hatch, Silverstone, Monza, Donnington Park, Monaco, Nurburgring)nn

meta.name: Chieftan
meta.desc: It's a fight to the finish in your armoured tank - fight the computer or a friend! As the mist clears on a new day, you, as tank commander, survey the terrain around you. Forests, mountains, remote chalets, a large lake and the enemy. Using your skill as a tank captain you must manoeuvre your tank through the terrain shooting trees and houses as you go. At the same time, the enemy tanks (controlled either by the computer or an adversary) will be in sight. You cannot travel across mountains or the lake, though you can shoot across the lake. You must now formulate your strategy: Do you clear an area to make engagement easier (and earn more points) by blasting the trees and chalets, or do you proceed directly to try and engage the enemy? Proceed, commander...

meta.name: Chip Buster
meta.desc:
In Chip Buster, the object is to keep your computer circuit board in working order by running about the tracks, repairing damaged components.
The damages ic caused by "bugs" which "byte" large holes in the board.

meta.name: Cholo
meta.desc:
Tras una guerra nuclear, los pocos supervivientes se unieron a la red de túneles subterráneos bajo la ciudad de Cholo, esperando el día en que bajara la radiación. Sin embargo, la radiación era más alta de lo esperado y el ordenador funcionó mal, atrapando a la gente bajo tierra. Debes enviar un grupo de droides a la superficie para desactivar el sistema.
Empiezas con un droide RAT llamado Rizzo, pero puedes encontrar y controlar a otros, incluidos droides aéreos. Cuando encuentres la sala de ordenadores, podrás conectar tu droide al terminal y ejecutar programas. Cuidado con los guardias robot que te disparan
Sólo una pequeña parte de Cholo es habitable, así que vigila el ruido de tu contador Geiger cuando te acerques a las zonas desérticas, ya que la radiación debilita a tu droide.

meta.name: Chrysalis
meta.desc: Mutant worms have invaded the cabbage patch and are out to get the caterpillars. As they chase you, they lay eggs which unless you eat them first, will hatch into more worms. The only chance you have is to frantically speed around the garden, in and out of the swinging gates and outwit the pursuing worms. If the green/blue eggs are eaten when blue, they are displayed at the bottom of the screen. Collect nine for an extra life. If "M"s are eaten when red, the multiplier at the bottom of the screen is increased and your score is increased by this number. If egg in the centre of the screen is eaten, the swinging gates lock and four flies are released but, as a bonus, your multiplier is increased. Skulls are lethal. Cabbages score 10 points Red eggs laid by worms score 10 points Green/blue eggs score 100 points

meta.name: Chuckie Egg
meta.desc:
En Chucky Egg, juegas como Harry el Gallinero, que debe recoger una docena de huevos en cada nivel, mientras evita a los pájaros. Los niveles se componen de plataformas, escaleras y ascensores, y son similares al juego de plataformas Donkey Kong. También se puede recoger maíz, para aumentar los puntos y detener la cuenta atrás durante un tiempo, aunque los pájaros también pueden comerse estas semillas.
Sólo hay 8 niveles distintos. El noveno nivel es una copia del nivel 1, el décimo es una copia del nivel 2, y así sucesivamente.

meta.name: Circus Games
meta.desc: Circus Games te convierte en la estrella del circo. Adiestramiento de tigres, trapecio, funambulismo y equitación: todos los trucos clásicos del circo requerirán la máxima concentración para satisfacer al público. Antes de embarcarte en una gira internacional, podrás entrenarte en los cuatro eventos para perfeccionar tus trucos.

meta.name: Citadel
meta.desc:
An old deserted castle is being used as a base for a dictator known as Marduk. It is being used to build a starport that will transport an alien invasion force to Earth. If this succeeds the dictator is likely to take control over the planet. The starport is powered by five crystals that can be found in the castle. The player's objective is to stop the invasion by destroying the starport. To do so he has to collect the crystals, activate the starport and travel to the alien homeworld. Once there the player has to find a figurine and use it to destroy the starport. After the invasion has been averted, the player can become the ruler of the citadel by collecting three hidden crowns and energy bottles.
Citadel is a platform game with a focus on puzzle solving. Gameplay involves exploring the citadel in search for the crystals while collecting items and avoiding enemies. The game does not have any instant deaths. Instead the player has an energy level that decreases when he comes into contact with enemies or hazards. However, if the player loses too much energy in a room he gets teleported back to the start of it. Energy is also lost continuously due to radiation so the player cannot stand around too long. The energy can be replenished by collecting bottles and solving puzzles. The game is low on combat as most enemies cannot be killed. The monks are an exception as they can be killed by shooting spells in their faces. The puzzle solving involves picking up objects and using them at the right location. The player can carry two objects at the same time. Examples of objects include keys to open doors, ice crystals that freezes water and trampolines that can be used to reach otherwise unreachable platforms.

meta.name: Citadel 2
meta.desc:
By popular demand, a brand-new arcade adventure from Superior Software. Explore the 150 action-packed screens, using the springs, moving platforms and reverse gravity, and solving many devious puzzles along the way.
The objective of the game is to find the five gems that are hidden around the castle, and take them to the teleporter to be destroyed.
To get you started, notice that the drawbridge is stuck and needs oiling, and the blue machine needs a cog!

meta.name: City Defence
meta.desc: City Defence is a game for one or two players. You are under attack. Deadly missiles are approaching your ground bases, from above. Each missile is capable of totally destroying its target. Your duty is to defend your cities from the waves of missiles by firing rockets from your three bases and steering them towards their targets. You may have up to five rockets at the air at once, but beware - you can only direct the last one fired, and you only have a limited number of rockets available for each wave. Luckily, between waves of missiles you get a chance to reload! To stay in the game, you must preserve at least one of your cities.

meta.name: Classic Adventure
meta.desc: Classic Adventure was the first major computer game and now it's become a classsic in its own time. This exciting new release of Classic Adventure for the Electron will enable you to slay dragons, find hidden treasures and bribe trolls: and as your skill increases through more and more victories and discoveries, you too can become a master adventurer. Before Crowther and Woods wrote ADVENTURE in 1978, games all had something more or less in common. The player knew the rules, and had to try to use them to get the best possible score. In an Adventure, the aim of the game if to discover the rules! To do this, the computer acts as your eyes and hands.

meta.name: Classic Arcade Games
meta.desc: It's a Database Publications' collection of four machine code arcade games: Snapman, Alien Intruders, Panzer Assault and Mayday (clones of Tetris, Hopper and Pacman)

meta.name: Clogger
meta.desc: In a strange and mystical land, many years ago, lived a peaceful race of beings known as the Cloggers. Cloggers were unusual creatures with three feet and no head but with strange powers that enabled them to perceive their environment in a way incomprehenisble to man. Cloggers also had a great love of art and, in order to achieve the status of Master Clogger, needed to prove their artistic appreciation. To do this, all young Cloggers had to undertake a series of cunningly conceived tests. These consisted of reassembling various pieces of artwork scattered around dangerous landscapes in the hidden valleys of Clogland. Few Cloggers ever reached the status of Master Clogger.

meta.name: Cluedo
meta.desc:
Cluedo is a version of the popular Waddington's board game known as Clue in the U.S.A.
The game is a detective / puzzle game for one to six players, though the computer will play five of these for a single player game. The objective is to determine who killed Dr Black, where they killed him, and what the murder weapon was.

meta.name: Codename: Droid
meta.desc:
Sequel of Stryker's Run game.
This took place in a multi-level underground base with many added moves and features. It is an early example of the Metroidvania genre

meta.name: Codename: Droid
meta.desc:
Sequel of Stryker's Run game.
This took place in a multi-level underground base with many added moves and features. It is an early example of the Metroidvania genre

meta.name: Colditz Adventure
meta.desc: An adventure game in which you play a prisoner of war who must escape from the famous prison Colditz.

meta.name: Colditz
meta.desc: Colditz asks you escaping from the German POW camp. The screen displays a map of the level you are on. Using the cursor keys, you can move around searching for escape routes and equipment. A door which is normally closed has a German guard outside. If you show him your pass, you may enter. Other doors need keys. There are eight escape routes, the most obvious being through the drains. When you have collected five essentials (e.g. compass) you can escape. However, trying to leave the prison with the wrong equipment leaves you with 30 days in the cooler. This is equivalent to a new start. Of course it's not that easy. Occasionally, an appel is called, and if you are caught for not appearing or you arrive with escape equipment you're in the cooler.

meta.name: Colony
meta.desc: Colony is based on LEMMINGS. There is a tribe of cute but stupid beings in this world need to escape from a variety of fates worse than death. It is your responsibility to lead them through puzzling scenes and seemingly solid surfaces to their version of Valhalla.

meta.name: Colossus Bridge 4
meta.desc: Colossus Bridge 4 is a bridge game for a single player (computer playing the other 3 hands; demo mode available). The game uses the Acol bidding system and Blackwood, Stayman, and Baron conventions; also supported are take-out doubles and the strong 2 club. Other features include hand input, save/load, rebid/replay, backstep, card autoplay/recommendation and cheat option.

meta.name: Combat Lynx
meta.desc:
Combat Lynx es un juego de simulación de combate de helicópteros bastante arcade.
La misión consiste en defender tus bases de los ataques enemigos de los alrededores. Para ello, tiene a su disposición toda una gama de armas: misiles autoguiados aire-aire, cañones y minas que lanzar. También hay que repostar.
Las misiones de combate darán paso ocasionalmente a misiones de asistencia para transportar al personal y repatriar a los heridos.
El mapa facilita la localización de los vehículos que se mezclan con el paisaje, así como la diferenciación de los vehículos aliados.
Varios niveles de dificultad mantienen el juego en marcha y te ayudan a olvidar algunos de los aspectos rutinarios de las misiones.

meta.name: Commando
meta.desc: The player takes control of a soldier named Super Joe, who starts by being dropped off in a jungle by a helicopter, and has to fight his way out singlehandedly, fending off a massive assault of enemy soldiers. Super Joe is armed with a sub-machine gun (which has unlimited ammunition) as well as a limited supply of hand grenades. While Joe can fire his gun in any of the eight directions that he faces, his grenades can only be thrown vertically towards the top of the screen, irrespective of the direction Joe is facing. Unlike his SMG bullets, grenades can be thrown to clear obstacles, and explosions from well placed grenades can kill several enemies at once. At the end of each level, the screen stops, and the player must fight several soldiers streaming from a gate or fortress. They are ordered out by a cowardly officer, who immediately runs away, although shooting him in the back awards the player bonus points. Along the way, one can attempt to free prisoners of war as they are transported across the screen by the enemy

meta.name: Commonwealth Games '86
meta.desc: Like so many other "Games" games, European Games is primarily a joystick-waggler, with some precision elements thrown in. Taking place over five or six disciplines, depending on platform, you generally go left to right (or hit the right keys) with your joystick on those judged on speed, while pressing the fire button once a metre reaches its top (as in golf games) is the general method for disciplines such as shooting.

meta.name: ZZZ(notgame):#NONGAME
meta.desc: Music Synthesiser turns the BBC Micro into a four channel sequencer, with three instrument channels and one effects channel. Facilities include: Entry and modification of musical pieces, Defining up to 16 instructions (only four available on OS 0.1), Saving and loading entire pieces on tape or disc, Playing pieces either straight through or using a verse structure. You are well advised to read the BBC manual's section on ENVELOPE before using Music Synthesiser.

meta.name: ZZZ(notgame):#NONGAME
meta.desc: Music Synthesiser turns the BBC Micro into a four channel sequencer, with three instrument channels and one effects channel. Facilities include: Entry and modification of musical pieces, Defining up to 16 instructions (only four available on OS 0.1), Saving and loading entire pieces on tape or disc, Playing pieces either straight through or using a verse structure. You are well advised to read the BBC manual's section on ENVELOPE before using Music Synthesiser.

p: Computer Maniacs Diary (1989)(Leisure Electronic Games)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Computer Maniacs Diary (1989)(Leisure Electronic Games)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.name: ZZZ(notgame):#NONGAME
meta.desc: Music Synthesiser turns the BBC Micro into a four channel sequencer, with three instrument channels and one effects channel. Facilities include: Entry and modification of musical pieces, Defining up to 16 instructions (only four available on OS 0.1), Saving and loading entire pieces on tape or disc, Playing pieces either straight through or using a verse structure. You are well advised to read the BBC manual's section on ENVELOPE before using Music Synthesiser.

meta.name: Condition Red
meta.desc: Set in a malfunctioning Plutonium Plant, your job is to collect 50 valuable plutonium crystals. The crystals are spread evenly across the ten chambers of the plant, but beware the various hazards :- collapsing floors, conveyor belts and escalators, mutilated life forms and your greatest problem, the limited air supply which is only replenished after completing each chamber. You have three lives, but you do gain an additional one after completing every second chamber.

meta.name: Confrontation
meta.desc: Confrontation is of two-player strategy games. When the game has loaded there is no scenario in the memory, so it can either be loaded from the tape or you can make up your own, which can also be saved. Each player's forces consists of 35 units, 12 separate types of unit, each conditions for victory are decided upon by the two players. For example, you may wish the outcome to be decided on the number of cities taken rather than units lost.

meta.name: Confuzion
meta.desc: Confuzion is a puzzle game developed and published by Incentive Software. The object is to guide a spark along a fuse wire. It is similar to the 1982 arcade game Loco-Motion. Confuzion was written by Paul Shirley who also wrote Spindizzy. The cassette tape on which the game was supplied also contained an audio track also titled "Confusion", which was composed by the Band Private Property (Matt Smith Lyrics, Joanne Holt/Steve Salt Music) and performed by Joanne Holt, Matt Smith, Steve Salt, Chris Weller and Gary Seaward. Rob Hubbard translated the original track into the game's soundtrack. The band persuaded Incentive Software to change their audio cassette duplication process from monophonic to stereophonic, so that the music could be better appreciated (mono was fine for the computer program data which was distributed on cassette tapes in the 80s).

meta.name: Conquering Everest
meta.desc: So you thought you could play? Just because you can waste a few galaxies full of aliens and get the frogs across the freeways pretty quick does *not* mean you're any good! Monsters, extra-terrestrials and crocodiles are easy meat next to the world's highest mountain. Instead of a laser you've got 34 Sherpas, 15 Porters and 18 Climbers ... and an awful lot of equipment! You can't kill a mountain, but can you climb it?

meta.name: Contract Bridge
meta.desc: Contract Bridge is a sophisticated bridge game for the single player, where all other three hands are bid and played by the computer. The ideal opportunity for uninterrupted bridge tuition or polishing up on your playing technique.

meta.name: Contraption
meta.desc: The zany professor needs golden apples for his machine, the "Contraption". Help him collect them all while avoiding bombs and other dangerous hazards!

p: Cook Beans on Toast! - Cooking Simulation (19xx)(D. Mann)[bootfile].zip
n: Cook Beans on Toast! - Cooking Simulation (19xx)(D. Mann)[bootfile]

meta.path: ./Cook Beans on Toast! - Cooking Simulation (19xx)(D. Mann)[bootfile].zip
meta.name: Cook Beans On Toast! - Cooking Simulation
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Cook Beans on Toast! - Cooking Simulation (19xx)(D. Mann)[bootfile]-marquee.png

meta.name: Cookie
meta.desc: The game puts you in control of Charlie the Chef, who keeps his ingredients locked in the larder. The ingredients can escape and bring out various nasties with them. Charlie must stun escaping ingredients with flour bombs and knock them into the mixing bowl, while ensuring the nasties get sent to the bins. Nice and simple stuff!

meta.name: Cops
meta.desc: A text adventure in which you, and a partner, must investigate the kidnapping of your police chief.

meta.name: Cops 'N Robbers
meta.desc: Light Fingers Lonegan is robbing the Acme Diamond Company, the world's largest supplier of uncut gems. The police are inside the office block, slowly combing each floor. Can 'Fingers' find all the diamonds and get back to his car or will he run out of ammunition and end up in jail? Search the Acme Diamond Company and steal the fortune in un-cut diamonds that are kept there. Many of the diamonds are in the mines, but there is a rumour that the mines are haunted. Unfortunately you have set off the automatic burgular alarm and the police are now searching the building. You have a limited supply of bullets for your 45 Magnum, so use them sparingly. Can you get the diamonds back to your car and collect more bullets? Can you find the combination to the safe? Will you be captured or even worse, killed?

meta.name: Copter Capers
meta.desc: Tu misión era rescatar a los supervivientes de un barco de pesca que se rompió en un mar embravecido. Sin embargo, no esperabas que un aumento repentino de las turbulencias te transportara de alguna manera a otra época, cuando no solo tenías que recoger a la gente, sino también evitar ser golpeado por naves enemigas de una generación anterior. Ahora estás en la batalla por el Atlántico, donde ya se están librando violentos combates.

meta.name: Corn Cropper
meta.desc: Corn Cropper is a simulation of a commercial wheat farm. You start with cash resources of £50,000, 30 acres of rich arable land, a tractor and two farm workers and are required to build up your assets to £250,000. Decisions on when to plant, irrigate, fertilise, spray and harvest are required. Cash resources are limited and therefore strategic decisions are required as to whether to buy seed corn, land or invest in farm machinery or whether to increase labour costs by employing more workers. The graphics include a Farm map and histograms. There are five levels of difficulty.

meta.name: Corporate Climber
meta.desc: Corporate Climber is a platform game programmed by J Hurwitz and Mark Yell. You have to traverse the levels, where there are men in lifts moving up and down the screen. You have to dodge them, and get across to the other side of the screen. You then move up one level, and so on, until you reach the top level.

meta.name: Cosmic Asteroids
meta.desc: Your craft has suddenly entered a menacing asteroid stormbelt and to come through safely, you must blast them to dust. Planet asteroids split to create even more danger, demanding faster blast power and quicker reflexes until all are destroyed. But watch out! For lurking in the shadows of darkness, the alien ships are waiting to pounce and halt your passage in this exciting journey across space.

meta.name: Cosmic Camouflage
meta.desc: The player controls a spaceship in order to avoid and destroy asteroids. The controls are rotate, thrust and fire. It expands on Meteors by introducing different types of asteroids and enemies, and a limited use camouflage function that allows the player to pass through asteroids and enemies.

meta.name: Cosmic Combat
meta.desc: Cosmic Combat is a two-player space conflict game with a range of options to suit your tastes. After reading the instructions contained within the game, you are asked to select either a colour or black and white display. The arena required can be selected by cycling through the ones available. The range of bullets can be set, enabling you to choose between close and long-range combat. A border can be included to delimit the area, otherwise a wrap-around effect operates. Combat then commences, and the person with the highest number of hits after 100 seconds is the winner.

meta.name: Cosmic Cruiser
meta.desc: A raiding party from the Rallom Empire has taken over one of Earth's far-flung space stations. The only Earth craft in the vicinity is an out-dated Cosmic Cruiser... and you are the pilot. A priority signal sends you on what could turn out to be a suicidal mission. You have been ordered to save the space station crew at all costs. As you hurtle towards your destiny, a daring plan forms in your mind. The only chance you have is to use your exterior laser cannon to blast open the station portals, then by making use of your body propulsion unit you will have to pluck the crewmen from the station and transport them back to your Cruiser. Your plan requires split-second timing and nerves of steel. As you transport your ward to the Cruiser, you must make snap decisions; if you use your hand laser, the blast will blow the crewmen into space. Can you get back to your laser cannon in time to blast the Rallom fighters or will you have to secure the rescued crewman to a nearby satellite to allow you to move more quickly? You see the space station in the distance, the adrenaline pumps through your body... Will you succeed?

meta.name: Cosmic Kidnap
meta.desc: Cosmic Kidnap is a variant of the arcade shoot 'em up Cosmic Guerilla. The objective of the game is to prevent aliens from freeing three captives from a prison. As the aliens appear from the sides the player has to shoot them down before they start tearing down the prison. If they manage to reach a prisoner they attempt to take it away and if they succeed the player loses one life. Once all the attackers have been killed the game moves on to the next level. For the third level a bug creature will appear. It is smart enough to follow the player and drop bombs and only a hit on its outer extremities will kill it. The game ends when the player has lost all three lives.
meta.developer: Julian Todd, Nick Wilson & Simon Eves

meta.name: Countdown
meta.desc:
Ever wanted to see how you would perform as a contestant on Countdown?
Well, here's your chance!
Thousands of words are contained in the program "dictionary", so prepare yourself for Countdown.

meta.name: Countdown To Doom
meta.desc: Countdown to Doom is a text adventure computer game, more recently termed interactive fiction, written in 1982 by Peter Killworth. Its setting is the planet Doomawangara, which is coyly said to be shortened to "Doom".

meta.name: Cowboy Shoot-Out
meta.desc: Welcome to the Wild West, where you have to be fast with the gun to stay alive. Each 'shootout' is fought with five lives each. You can choose to play against the computer or against a friend by selecting 1 or 2 players. The one player option gives the computer the other gun, and asks what level you want the computer to play at. Level 1 is easy; Level 10 is not! The two opponents are on either side of the screen with waggons between them which roll up and down the screen. The object is to shoot your opponent four times, whoever does this first is the winner.

meta.name: Crack-Up
meta.desc: Crack-Up es un clon de Breakout para 1 o 2 jugadores y cuenta con 32 pantallas llenas de acción. El objetivo del juego es simplemente destruir cada muro de ladrillos y pasar a la siguiente pantalla. Pero cuidado, algunos ladrillos necesitan más de un golpe para destruirse y otros no pueden destruirse en absoluto. Para hacer el juego aún más desafiante, algunos ladrillos son invisibles. Actúan del mismo modo que los ladrillos normales (es decir, algunos pueden destruirse y otros son indestructibles), salvo que no puedes verlos. Hay 7 ladrillos de bonificación que aparecen aleatoriamente y que permiten, por ejemplo, pasar directamente al siguiente nivel, duplicar el daño, ralentizar la bola...

meta.name: Cranky
meta.desc: Cranky is a single player maths-based educational game. Cranky is a calculator who, naturally enough, has problems when it rains because the water gets into his circuits and damages them. The object of the game is to repair Cranky's circuits but, because the rain has caused damage, only the keys 3 and 5 are working. In the 'Help Cranky' game there are three levels of difficulty, all of which play the same. Cranky will show which of the 100 circuits are damaged on a numbered grid and the player's task is to find ways to make up the damaged numbers. Starting with just the 3 and the 5 keys working the player can create the numbers 3, 5, 33, 35, 53, & 55. These numbers can be added and subtracted to make other numbers and once a player has entered 5 - 3 = 2 for example, the number 2 becomes available and can be used in future sums. Once all the circuits have been repaired Cranky becomes available to use as a functioning on-screen calculator. In the 'Play Challenge' game the player selects a pair of numbers in the range 1 - 9 and then uses these in a 'Help Cranky' game. The game documentation does warn that not all number pairs will allow the player to successfully complete the grid.

meta.name: Crawler
meta.desc: A version of the popular Centipede, featuring a very smooth-scrolling centipede.

meta.name: Crazee Rider
meta.desc: Crazee Rider is the spiritual successor to Superior Software's Overdrive. Originally envisioned as a car racing game it was turned into a motorcycle racer during development. Players need to race opponents over different tracks, starting out at the 60th position. To progress the player has to end in the top six of the pack in a single lap. Compared to Overdrive there are more opportunities to avoid opponents and tracks also have bends. Two keys are used to speed up or down and two others to steer. For bonus points it is possible to bump into the sides of other opponents. This does not slow them down, it only provides points. Driving straight into them from the back slows down the player's motorcycle significantly. This also happens when the curb is hit while taking corners at a high speed. A mini-map of the track is shown near the top of the screen, along with the score. The current position and the speed is shown at the bottom.

meta.name: Crazy Er*Bert
meta.desc: Crazy Erbert is a Qbert type game. The aim is to visit each cube so that its top surface changes colour. At the start you can choose from 10 levels of difficulty and whether movement should be fast or slow. Erbert is persistently chased by Coily Snake and Boris Gorilla. If they catch you one of your three lives is lost. Escape is provided by two elevators which return you to the surface. If Boris or Coily try to follow, they vanish for a few seconds.

meta.name: Crazy Painter
meta.desc: The objective of the game is to paint round all the squares avoiding the pursuing tribesmen or teddy-bears. However, if you paint-in all four corner squares, you will be able to chase and catch the tribesmen or teddy-bears for a limited period of time. On the bonus screen, the aim is to start the monkey moving down the correct line to collect his bananas.

meta.name: Crazy Tracer
meta.desc: Crazy Tracer is a clone of Amidar. The player controls a paint brush and needs to paint levels divided into rectangles of various sizes. They are painted by moving over the bordering edges, colouring them. When a rectangle is traced entirely, it is filled with a colour. Meanwhile enemies that roam the edges need to be avoided. Sometimes fruit appears in the middle of a rectangle for a short moment. When the rectangle is painted while the fruit is still inside, additional points are earned. The character has a number of lives and the game keeps tracks of the score.

p: Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
n: Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile]

meta.path: ./Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile].zip
meta.name: Cred Breaks Out
meta.desc: Cred must rescue the Interrestials from their Ontick prison by touching them and leading them back to his spaceship. When an Ontick touches Cred, you lose a life. Touch the Fire button to block the Ontick's way with a Trustcard - but be careful, for Cred's way will be blocked too! When an Ontick touches the Interrestials, they will disperse. If you succeed in taking the Interrestials back to Cred's spaceship, you will pass through a succession of different screens. You have three minutes of oxygen.
meta.image: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile]-image.jpg
meta.marquee: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile]-marquee.png
meta.thumbnail: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile]-thumb.jpg
meta.boxback: Z:/Digistorage/Arley4dCloudMedia/bbcmicro/images/Cred Breaks Out! (1986)(TSB Magic Micro Club)[h TSTH][bootfile]-boxback.jpg

meta.name: Creepy Cave
meta.desc: Vampire bats, ectoplasm bolts, slime covered ledges and acid pools. All these and more must be faced if our mega hero, Dirk Daring, is to successfully negotiate the depths of Creepy Cave and recover his door key from the evil ghost. Dirk Daring, adventurer, explorer, space pilot and top goal scorer for Manchester United, was returning home after winning the world karate championships, when an evil ghost stole his front door key and vanished into the depths of Creepy Cave. Leaping from ledge to ledge, avoiding vampire bats and ectoplasm bolts, Dirk takes off in pursuit. There is only one way through each cavern, and only one exit. Collecting the crucifix from each screen will earn extra points but be careful as there is a limited air supply. Can you help our mega hero to recover his key and get home in time for supper?

meta.name: Cribbage
meta.desc: A computer version of the pub card game Cribbage in which you play against the computer.

meta.name: Cricket
meta.desc: It is summer and the village cricket season is in full swing. A touring team (The Merry Hackers Circus) has been challenging teams from the local pubs. The regulars at the 'Frog and Ferret', 'Pig and Whistle' and even the 'Brahms and Liszt' have hopelessly lost. Can you succeed where others have failed?

meta.name: Howszat!
meta.desc: Be a cricket star and enjoy all the hazards of cricket to beat your equally keen opponent.

meta.name: Croaker
meta.desc: The objective in Croaker is to get your family of frogs safely across the busy motorway and over a polluted river into each of the lairs set in the embankment. To do this you must dodge between the traffic, then leap from logs to turtles without falling in. When all five lairs have been filled you move onto a higher phase. The roads become busier, some logs turn into crocodiles, and the turtles sometimes dive, making the whole exercise much trickier. You can jump onto a crocodile's back, but beware his jaws!

meta.name: Croakit
meta.desc: Probably the most exciting version of the well-known arcade game, Croakit from Kerian faithfully reproduces all the fun of being a frog on the BBC Model B microcomputer! Jump your five frogs over a motorway and lake to the safety of their homes.

meta.name: Crosswords
meta.desc: A selection of simple crosswords to complete, aimed at ages 8+.

meta.name: Cruncher
meta.desc: You must trample on the flashing time bombs to score points. The points scored are equal to 100 times the number shown. If the number drops to zero then the bomb will bomb you!!! Death will also afflict you if you walk over a skull or walk into a boot.

meta.name: Crypt Capers
meta.desc: Well are you lucky! One day while you are studying ancient Egyptian ruins, you discover an unknown pyramid buried in the sands of the Sahara. Once inside, you are standing in a chamber containing several treasures. What more could you wish? Perhaps you would wish that there were not as many snakes and fireballs around. Now, to get deeper into the pyramid you must collect the keys you find and unlock the doors. Sounds easy? Well, beware of the Triads and shrinking ghosts. Anyhow, you have your gun and it should not be impossible to reach the Pharos chamber on level twelve. On your way you will find old jars, statues, papyrus roots and other valuable items. Oh, just one more thing to remember.

meta.name: Crystal Castles
meta.desc: Bentley Bear está atrapado en los castillos de cristal. Para salir, debe recoger todas las gemas que se encuentran en cada castillo. El juego se desarrolla en una falsa vista isométrica en 3D a través de castillos multiplataforma. Cuando Bentley recoge todas las gemas del nivel, pasa al siguiente castillo, más difícil. Bentley tendrá que evitar una gran variedad de criaturas peligrosas que deambulan por los niveles. De vez en cuando aparecerá un sombrero mágico. Si Bentley recoge el sombrero, será temporalmente invencible.

meta.name: Crystal Castles
meta.desc: Bentley Bear está atrapado en los castillos de cristal. Para salir, debe recoger todas las gemas que se encuentran en cada castillo. El juego se desarrolla en una falsa vista isométrica en 3D a través de castillos multiplataforma. Cuando Bentley recoge todas las gemas del nivel, pasa al siguiente castillo, más difícil. Bentley tendrá que evitar una gran variedad de criaturas peligrosas que deambulan por los niveles. De vez en cuando aparecerá un sombrero mágico. Si Bentley recoge el sombrero, será temporalmente invencible.

meta.name: Cube Master
meta.desc: Cube Master is a digital version of the Rubik's Cube puzzle game. The screen is split in two areas. Bottom half has a graphical presentation of the cube viewed from two different perspectives with all of its faces visible. The top half is used to enter text commands to interact with the cube. Each face has a letter and by entering it that face is turned 90 degrees clockwise while entering it with shift held down turns it 90 degrees anti-clockwise. To scramble the cube the player enters X followed by the number of moves to shuffle. It's also possible to have the computer solve the cube by typing S. There's also a feature for the player to draw his own cube by entering the colour wanted for each square.

p: Curse of the Middle Kingdom, The (1983)(Pro)[h TSTH][bootfile-E00DFS].zip
n: Curse of the Middle Kingdom, The (1983)(Pro)[h TSTH][bootfile-E00DFS]
